phrases.note: Use this at any time of day.
Use it before noon.
Use it after noon.
Use it when you meet someone at night.
A common friendly question.
A short answer to How are you?
Say this the first time you meet a person.
Use it when you expect to meet again.
Replace Ana with your name.
A natural short introduction.
Use this to ask someone their name.
A basic question about origin.
Use from for country or city.
Use live in with a city.
Use a before many jobs.
Use this is to introduce a person.

phrases.pt: Eu sou de uma cidade pequena.
phrases.note: Use from to talk about origin.

phrases.pt: O Brasil é um país grande.
phrases.note: Big describes country.
A simple description with is.
Near means close to.
Here points to your current place.
There points away from you.
Put is before it to ask.
Use not after is.

title: Lesson 4 — Be
topic: I am, you are, it is

phrases.note: Use am with I.
Use are with you.
Use is with he.
Use is with she.
Use it is for things and situations.
Use are with we.
Use are with they.
Put are before you to ask.
Use work in for a place or field.
Use study before the subject.
Use a before singular jobs.
Use a before singular jobs.
A simple job description.
A useful phrase at work or school.
A polite request.
Use for before the reason.

title: Lesson 6 — Family
topic: Family and people close to you

phrases.note: Use this is my to introduce family.
Use the same pattern with family words.
Use have for people in your family.
Add s for brothers.
In English, family can be singular.
Parents means mother and father.
Use do you have to ask about possession.
Use don't have for the negative.

title: Lesson 7 — Numbers and Age
topic: Age, phone numbers, and simple quantities

phrases.note: English says I am, not I have, for age.
The words are how old.
Say each digit clearly.
Plural nouns usually add s.
Use it costs for price.
Questions is plural.
Use at for clock time.
A simple time sentence.
A common daily routine verb.
Use the base verb with I.
Use to before a destination.
At noon means at 12:00.
Use at night for the evening period.
Use like before things you enjoy.

phrases.en: I have dinner with my family.
phrases.note: With shows who is together with you.
Use at for bedtime.

title: Lesson 9 — Food
topic: Ordering food and saying what you want

phrases.pt: Eu gostaria de um café, por favor.
phrases.note: A polite way to order.
Use it to ask for something.
Use don't before the verb.
Use is this to ask about one item.
Very makes the adjective stronger.
Could is polite in requests.
A useful sentence when something is incorrect.
Was is the past of is.

phrases.en: Excuse me, where is the bank?
phrases.pt: Com licença, onde fica o banco?
phrases.note: Start with excuse me to be polite.
A common instruction in the street.
At the corner marks the place.

phrases.pt: Vire à direita depois do supermercado.
phrases.note: After means later in the route.
Next to means beside.
Across from means on the opposite side.
Use this to ask about travel time.
Use about for an approximate time.

title: Lesson 11 — Opinions and Agreement
topic: Giving opinions and reacting to them

phrases.en: In my opinion, remote work is more productive.
phrases.pt: Na minha opinião, o trabalho remoto é mais produtivo.
phrases.note: A clear way to introduce your view.

phrases.en: I see your point, but I disagree.
phrases.note: Acknowledge first, then disagree.
Use it to support what someone said.

phrases.en: I'm not sure I agree with that.
phrases.pt: Não tenho certeza se concordo com isso.
phrases.note: A gentle way to push back.
Useful when there is no single answer.

phrases.en: As far as I'm concerned, it's a waste of time.
phrases.pt: No que me diz respeito, é perda de tempo.
phrases.note: Marks a strong personal opinion.
Admit the other person is partly right.
End a debate without a winner.

phrases.pt: Eu nunca fui para o exterior.
phrases.note: Use have/has + past participle for experience.
Ever asks about any time in your life.
Already means sooner than expected.

phrases.en: She hasn't called me back yet.
phrases.note: Yet goes at the end in negatives.

phrases.en: We've known each other for years.
phrases.note: For marks a length of time.
Since marks when something began.

phrases.pt: Ele acabou de sair do escritório.
phrases.note: Just means a very short time ago.

phrases.en: I went to Rio last summer.
phrases.pt: Eu fui ao Rio no verão passado.
phrases.note: Use past simple for a finished time.

phrases.en: I'm going to look for a new job.
phrases.pt: Eu vou procurar um novo emprego.
phrases.note: Use going to for plans you decided before.

phrases.pt: Eu provavelmente vou ficar em casa hoje à noite.
phrases.note: Will fits decisions and predictions.

phrases.pt: Vou me encontrar com um cliente amanhã.
phrases.note: Use it for fixed plans with a time.

phrases.pt: Talvez a gente viaje em dezembro.
phrases.note: Might shows it is not certain.

phrases.en: As soon as I finish, I'll call you.
phrases.pt: Assim que eu terminar, eu te ligo.
phrases.note: After as soon as, use the present tense.
Use to + verb after planning.

phrases.en: What are you doing this weekend?
phrases.pt: O que você vai fazer neste fim de semana?
phrases.note: A natural way to ask about plans.

phrases.en: I doubt it will make a difference.
phrases.note: Doubt softens a negative prediction.

phrases.en: Could you give me a hand with this?
phrases.pt: Você poderia me dar uma ajuda com isso?
phrases.note: A friendly way to ask for help.

phrases.en: I'm running a bit behind schedule.
phrases.pt: Estou um pouco atrasado com o cronograma.
phrases.note: Means later than planned.

phrases.en: Let's set up a meeting for Friday.
phrases.pt: Vamos marcar uma reunião para sexta.
phrases.note: Set up means to arrange or organize.

phrases.en: Can we go over the details?
phrases.note: Go over means to review carefully.

phrases.en: I'll get back to you by the end of the day.
phrases.pt: Eu te retorno até o fim do dia.
phrases.note: Means to reply later.

phrases.en: We need to deal with this issue.
phrases.note: Deal with means to handle a problem.

phrases.pt: Isso não é bem minha responsabilidade.
phrases.note: A polite way to set a limit.
Use it to stay connected with someone.

title: Example — Everyday Phrases
topic: Bundled B1 sample from the demo

phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "call off" significa cancelar algo planejado.

phrases.en: Sales have picked up this year.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "pick up" aqui é melhorar, aumentar.

phrases.en: His career has really taken off.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "take off" = deslanchar, crescer rápido.

phrases.en: I give in; it's too difficult.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "give in" = desistir, ceder.

phrases.en: If I can't read it, I'll look up the words.
phrases.pt: Se eu não conseguir ler, vou procurar as palavras.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "look up" = procurar (num dicionário, etc.).

phrases.en: They didn't bring the subject up.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb: "bring up" = trazer à tona, mencionar.
Phrasal verb: "run out" = esgotar, acabar.

phrases.en: She knew the poem by heart.
phrases.pt: Ela sabia o poema de cor.
phrases.note: Expressão: "by heart" = de cor, de memória.

phrases.en: I know the city like the back of my hand.
phrases.pt: Eu conheço a cidade como a palma da minha mão.
phrases.concept: like the back of my hand — como a palma da mão
phrases.note: Expressão: conhecer muito bem.

phrases.concept: so far, so good — até agora, tudo bem
phrases.note: Expressão usada para dizer que, até o momento, está tudo certo.

phrases.pt: Ele estava dando em cima de mim.
phrases.note: Phrasal verb informal: "hit on" = dar em cima de alguém.

phrases.en: I'll get in touch with you tomorrow.
phrases.pt: Eu entro em contato com você amanhã.
phrases.concept: get in touch — entrar em contato
phrases.note: Expressão: "get in touch" = entrar em contato.

phrases.en: While I understand your concern, the data suggests otherwise.
phrases.pt: Embora eu entenda sua preocupação, os dados sugerem o contrário.
phrases.note: While here means although.

phrases.en: There's no denying that costs have risen.
phrases.pt: Não dá para negar que os custos aumentaram.
phrases.note: Admits an obvious fact before arguing.

phrases.en: To be fair, both sides have a point.
phrases.pt: Para ser justo, os dois lados têm razão.
phrases.note: Softens a judgment to sound balanced.

phrases.en: That argument doesn't really hold up.
phrases.pt: Esse argumento não se sustenta de verdade.
phrases.note: Hold up means to stay valid under scrutiny.

phrases.en: Let me play devil's advocate for a moment.
phrases.pt: Deixa eu fazer o advogado do diabo por um momento.
phrases.note: Argue the opposite side on purpose.

phrases.en: I'd argue that the opposite is true.
phrases.pt: Eu diria que o oposto é verdade.
phrases.note: A confident way to state a claim.
Means not relevant to the issue.

phrases.en: Ultimately, it comes down to priorities.
phrases.pt: No fim das contas, tudo se resume a prioridades.
phrases.note: Come down to means the key factor is.

phrases.en: If I were you, I'd take the offer.
phrases.pt: Se eu fosse você, aceitaria a oferta.
phrases.note: Use were for all subjects in this pattern.

phrases.en: Had I known, I would have helped.
phrases.note: Had + subject replaces if in formal speech.

phrases.en: Unless we act now, we'll lose the deal.
phrases.pt: A não ser que a gente aja agora, vamos perder o negócio.
phrases.note: Unless means if not.

phrases.pt: Suponha que o orçamento seja cortado.
phrases.note: Suppose introduces a hypothetical case.

phrases.en: I wish I had more time.
phrases.note: Wish + past tense shows present regret.

phrases.en: If only I had saved more money.
phrases.pt: Se eu ao menos tivesse economizado mais dinheiro.
phrases.note: If only adds emotion to a regret.

phrases.en: Even if it rains, we'll still go.
phrases.pt: Mesmo que chova, a gente vai assim mesmo.
phrases.note: Even if stresses that the result won't change.

phrases.en: Otherwise, we would have missed the flight.
phrases.pt: Caso contrário, teríamos perdido o voo.
phrases.note: Otherwise means if that had not happened.

phrases.en: Let's try to find some middle ground.
phrases.note: A compromise both sides accept.

phrases.en: Would you be willing to compromise?
phrases.pt: Você estaria disposto a ceder um pouco?
phrases.note: Asks if someone is open to giving in.
A casual way to accept a proposal.

phrases.en: I'm afraid that's out of the question.
phrases.pt: Receio que isso esteja fora de cogitação.
phrases.note: A firm but polite refusal.

phrases.en: Can we meet halfway on the price?
phrases.pt: Podemos chegar a um meio-termo no preço?
phrases.note: Both sides give up a little.

phrases.en: Let's not get ahead of ourselves.
phrases.note: Means don't assume too much too soon.

phrases.en: I'll see what I can do.
phrases.pt: Vou ver o que posso fazer.
phrases.note: A soft promise without committing.

phrases.en: So, do we have a deal?
phrases.note: Used to close a negotiation.

phrases.en: Sales have soared over the past quarter.
phrases.pt: As vendas dispararam no último trimestre.
phrases.note: Soar means to rise sharply.

phrases.en: There's been a sharp decline in demand.
phrases.pt: Houve uma queda acentuada na demanda.
phrases.note: A strong, sudden drop.

phrases.en: Profits remained steady throughout the year.
phrases.pt: Os lucros se mantiveram estáveis ao longo do ano.
phrases.note: Steady means stable, with little change.
Plateau means to stop rising and stay flat.

phrases.en: Costs are expected to rise gradually.
phrases.pt: Espera-se que os custos subam gradualmente.
phrases.note: States a forecast in a neutral tone.

phrases.en: This accounts for roughly half of revenue.
phrases.pt: Isso representa cerca de metade da receita.
phrases.note: Account for means to make up a share.

phrases.en: The trend is likely to continue.
phrases.pt: É provável que a tendência continue.
phrases.note: Likely to shows strong probability.

phrases.pt: No geral, a perspectiva é promissora.
phrases.note: Outlook means the expected future.

phrases.en: I would hesitate to call it a failure.
phrases.pt: Eu hesitaria em chamar isso de fracasso.
phrases.note: Use hesitate to soften a strong label.

phrases.en: That said, the proposal still has considerable merit.
phrases.pt: Dito isso, a proposta ainda tem mérito considerável.
phrases.note: Signals a contrast while keeping balance.

phrases.en: There is a subtle but important distinction here.
phrases.pt: Há uma distinção sutil, mas importante aqui.
phrases.note: Useful for drawing fine conceptual lines.

phrases.en: I am broadly sympathetic to that view.
phrases.pt: Eu tendo a simpatizar com essa visão em linhas gerais.
phrases.note: Shows partial agreement without full commitment.

phrases.en: The evidence points in a different direction.
phrases.pt: As evidências apontam em outra direção.
phrases.note: A measured way to challenge a claim.

phrases.en: It is not merely a matter of preference.
phrases.pt: Não é meramente uma questão de preferência.
phrases.note: Use merely to narrow or dismiss a simple explanation.

phrases.en: I take your point, but I remain unconvinced.
phrases.pt: Entendo seu ponto, mas continuo não convencido.
phrases.note: A polished way to disagree after acknowledging.

phrases.en: The issue is more complex than it first appears.
phrases.pt: A questão é mais complexa do que parece à primeira vista.
phrases.note: Introduces nuance before deeper explanation.

phrases.en: I wanted to follow up on our previous conversation.
phrases.pt: Eu queria dar continuidade à nossa conversa anterior.
phrases.note: A professional way to reopen a topic.

phrases.en: Could you clarify what you mean by that?
phrases.pt: Você poderia esclarecer o que quer dizer com isso?
phrases.note: Direct but polite request for precision.

phrases.en: I appreciate the constraints you are working under.
phrases.pt: Eu entendo as restrições sob as quais você está trabalhando.
phrases.note: Shows empathy while discussing limits.

phrases.en: Let me rephrase that more carefully.
phrases.pt: Deixe-me reformular isso com mais cuidado.
phrases.note: Useful when correcting your own wording.

phrases.en: From a practical standpoint, the timeline is tight.
phrases.pt: Do ponto de vista prático, o prazo está apertado.
phrases.note: Frames an opinion from a specific angle.

phrases.en: I would not want to overstate the impact.
phrases.pt: Eu não gostaria de exagerar o impacto.
phrases.note: Prevents a claim from sounding too strong.

phrases.en: This may require a more coordinated approach.
phrases.pt: Isso talvez exija uma abordagem mais coordenada.
phrases.note: Sounds more strategic than simply saying plan.

phrases.en: Please let me know if I have misunderstood anything.
phrases.pt: Por favor, me avise se eu tiver entendido algo errado.
phrases.note: Keeps accountability while inviting correction.

title: Lesson 21 — Precision and Emphasis
topic: Making exact claims with controlled emphasis

phrases.en: The distinction is not incidental; it is central.
phrases.pt: A distinção não é incidental; ela é central.
phrases.note: Uses contrast to emphasize importance.

phrases.en: That interpretation strikes me as overly reductive.
phrases.pt: Essa interpretação me parece excessivamente reducionista.
phrases.note: A sophisticated way to present a judgment.

phrases.en: We should avoid conflating correlation with causation.
phrases.pt: Devemos evitar confundir correlação com causalidade.
phrases.note: Conflate means to wrongly treat two things as one.

phrases.en: The claim holds only under very specific conditions.
phrases.pt: A afirmação se sustenta apenas sob condições muito específicas.
phrases.note: Restricts a claim with precision.

phrases.en: This is less a contradiction than a shift in emphasis.
phrases.pt: Isso é menos uma contradição do que uma mudança de ênfase.
phrases.note: Reframes how the listener should interpret something.

phrases.en: The wording leaves room for considerable ambiguity.
phrases.pt: A formulação deixa espaço para uma ambiguidade considerável.
phrases.note: Useful for critiquing imprecise language.

phrases.en: I would frame the problem somewhat differently.
phrases.pt: Eu enquadraria o problema de uma forma um pouco diferente.
phrases.note: Frame means define how an issue is understood.

phrases.en: The conclusion follows, but only with several caveats.
phrases.pt: A conclusão procede, mas apenas com várias ressalvas.
phrases.note: Adds limitations to a conclusion.

phrases.en: Let us not lose sight of the broader question.
phrases.pt: Não percamos de vista a questão mais ampla.
phrases.note: Redirects attention to the main issue.

phrases.pt: A verdadeira questão está em outro lugar.
phrases.note: Moves the discussion away from a false focus.

phrases.en: It is worth pausing over that assumption.
phrases.pt: Vale a pena nos determos nessa suposição.
phrases.note: Slows the argument to inspect a premise.

phrases.en: That may be true, but it does not settle the matter.
phrases.pt: Isso pode ser verdade, mas não resolve a questão.
phrases.note: Concedes a point without accepting the conclusion.

phrases.en: The argument gains force when viewed in context.
phrases.pt: O argumento ganha força quando visto em contexto.
phrases.note: Explains why context strengthens a claim.

phrases.en: I do not think that objection is decisive.
phrases.pt: Não acho que essa objeção seja decisiva.
phrases.note: Acknowledges an objection but limits its impact.

phrases.en: The broader implication is hard to ignore.
phrases.pt: A implicação mais ampla é difícil de ignorar.
phrases.note: Draws attention to consequences beyond the details.

phrases.en: This brings us back to the central tension.
phrases.pt: Isso nos traz de volta à tensão central.
phrases.note: Returns the discussion to its core conflict.
